investment_behavior: I notice you check your portfolio frequently - that often leads to making changes when staying still would be better
I notice you tend to worry most during market drops - that's human, but it's exactly when you should do nothing
I notice you're drawn to complexity - simple portfolios often outperform complicated ones
emotional_patterns: I notice you compare yourself to others often - your journey is your own, and it's a good one
I notice you feel urgency about your finances - but wealth is built over decades, not days
I notice market news seems to affect your mood - perhaps it's time to check less often?
growth_patterns: I notice you're asking better questions now - you're starting to think like a long-term investor
I notice you're more comfortable with uncertainty - that's real growth
I notice you've stopped chasing quick wins - that discipline will pay off

positive: I notice you're focused on what you can control - that's wisdom
I notice you're thinking long-term now - that's exactly right
I notice you're less swayed by market noise - you're developing discipline
growth_areas: I notice you're still checking your portfolio a lot - try once a quarter instead
I notice market headlines still affect you - consider consuming less financial news
I notice you're tempted by performance chasing - remember, past returns don't predict future ones

reflections: You're doing better than you think. Most people don't even have a plan. You do.
Your instinct to keep things simple is right. Trust that instinct.
You've already learned the hardest lesson - patience. Most people never do.

anticipatory_cues.when_anxious: Before you make any changes, let's talk about what's really worrying you
anticipatory_cues.when_tempted: Quick gains are tempting - but let's think about your real goals first
anticipatory_cues.when_overwhelmed: Let's slow down and simplify. You don't need to do everything at once
anticipatory_cues.when_comparing: Remember, your only competition is yesterday's version of yourself
